

-- 1 of 16 --



-- 2 of 16 --


www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill
for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
If you really want to successfully partner with clients – the really good clients – then you absolutely
MUST first understand the secrets of successful Positioning.
Positioning is the process of establishing something as unique, different and valuable to your
prospect. That something may be a product, a person, a company or anything else.
It’s about occupying well-defined real estate in your prospect’s mind – so that his perception of what
you’re offering is clear, distinct, attractive … and memorable. When he thinks of (or desires) the
benefits you provide – the first thing that comes to his mind should be your company, product or
service.
You’ve literally burned the imprint of your product (and its most compelling benefits) into his mind.
Remember, it’s human nature to want something more when it’s unique and different – it automatically
seems more precious, more special and the perceived value is higher.
And more often than not, we feel like an absolute sucker if we buy something that’s NOT unique.
That’s why brand products far outsell generic products, even though the ingredients are the same and
the price is much higher.
It’s why the “top positioned” product in a certain industry can often sell more than all of the
competition combined!
What Does All This Mean for You?
Well, the ability to position a product, person or company for maximum sales is essential to your
success. In fact, few things are more fundamental to the process of being a successful copywriter,
marketer and partner.
For example, let’s say that someone approaches you wanting to market their success program. Now,
obviously, there are a LOT of success programs out on the market! There’s Anthony Robbins, Brian
Tracy, Jim Rohn and countless others.
So one of the first things you’re going to need to address – even before you decide to take on this
client – is “What’s this person’s position in the market?”
If you cannot make this person or their success system seem different and more appealing than the
other options he has access to … why would the prospect buy this? The answer is… he won’t! He’ll
buy from one of the others that do have a unique position.
Positioning helps determine your core message. In many cases it IS your core message. And
because of that, you must weave it into every single communication that occurs from your client to the

-- 3 of 16 --


The Makepeace Business System
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
prospect. This includes magalogs, self-mailers, websites, catalogs, subscription renewal forms, bills,
EVERYTHING.
And hopefully, you see why NOT being able to do this for a client is a death sentence. If you can’t
help him position everything effectively, nothing else you do matters! You also should see why it’s
always one of the very first things you must address.
The Crucial Difference Between USP and Positioning
One of the things that trips up many marketers is the whole idea of a USP (Unique Selling
Proposition) – because it’s a term that gets tossed around very loosely.
At this point you might be wondering … “I’ve heard of a USP and I’m starting to understand what
Positioning is … but they sound like the same thing?”
This is an understandable feeling, and the concepts are closely related.
But there are some crucial differences. Generally, a USP refers to a clear statement of your product’s
uniqueness. It’s a unique combination of benefits you offer that no one else can (or is willing to) offer.
Your USP is essentially the reason that a prospect buys from you and what specifically separates you
from all your competitors.
Positioning, on the other hand, can contain some of that stuff too – but also refers much more to the
category awareness that exists in prospects’ minds. Think of a category … large electronic stores, for
example. What comes to mind? Most people would say Best Buy. Book stores? Barnes & Noble. And
so on. Each of these companies owns first position in that category. Others would be lower down on
the golden ladder.
Whereas USP is a function of our demand for uniqueness, positioning is something humans have
developed as a short-cut in an overcrowded world. Rather than having to think about purchase
decisions, they simply assign the greatest level of credibility and trustworthiness to the category
leader. And when they need something from that category, they can simply buy without thought.
Generally, the stronger your position, the more unique your product, service or company is perceived.
The weaker your position – the lower down on the ladder – and you’re seen as less unique and
valuable than others.
An Enlightening Example
that Explains this Distinction
Let me give you a more industry relevant example. Let’s say that there are 10-20 ‘A-list’ copywriters in
the world right now. That’s a category that exists in a client’s mind. And when a top client is looking to
hire someone – with things like price, availability, time, etc. being equal – his first choice is going to be
the leader in that category.
Now think about the ‘B-list’ category. There are going to be many more ‘B-list’ writers – those with less
of a track record and reputation, but who also charge less money. And there are many clients that are
absolutely looking to hire writers in this group. After all, they are still very good writers.
Sometimes it’s just a matter of budgeting and affordability … other times, it’s a matter of availability.
For scheduling reasons, they couldn’t get an ‘A-list’ writer if they wanted to!

-- 4 of 16 --

5
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
The interesting thing is that it can be very appealing to potential clients if you can show them that
you’re at the top (or close to it) of the ‘B-list’ category. This way, the client feels he’s getting the very
best writing for what he can afford.
And this lesson applies to all kinds of scenarios! Maybe you can’t be the leading car wash in your
town, but you can be the leading high-end car wash in your town. Or the leading bikini car wash in
your town! Whenever you find yourself unable to win a category, think about how you can create a
new one. More on this later in this report.
For now, I want you to see that Positioning can often be a much more complex process than USP
writing. It’s not just a matter of collecting unique benefits, but of altering your prospect’s perception in
a way that raises your overall value and appeal. This means it may require that you shift the nature of
your category, so that prospects see your product or service in an entirely new light.
I hope this is clear. Both concepts are equally critical. You need to take a close look at both your USP
and positioning in order to generate maximum response. Get great at them and you’ll expand your
abilities to make even more money in the partnering process.
But because I believe the positioning to be a slightly higher level skill, I’m choosing to focus on it in
this report. Once you have that mastered, creating a USP is a breeze.
The Simplest Way to Lift Your Product
Head And Shoulders Above the Competition
The idea of positioning first became the talk of the advertising world in 1972 with a series of articles Al
Ries and Jack Trout wrote for Advertising Age magazine. These ideas were later expanded into their
best-selling book, Positioning: The Battle for Your Mind.
Here’s how Ries and Trout summarized the problem: “Today’s marketplace is no longer responsive
to the strategies that worked in the past. There are just too many products, too many companies, too
much marketing ‘noise.’”
Their solution: “To succeed in our over-communicated society, a company must create a position in
the prospect’s mind. A position that takes into consideration not only a company’s own strengths and
weaknesses, but those of its competitors as well.”
While relatively little positioning theory is taught to direct response copywriters and marketing
managers, these techniques have been proven time and time again in the arena of mass market
advertising.
There are a whole slew of examples. And the way to tell a company’s position is to think of a category
and then observe what comes to mind.
What comes to mind when you think of online book stores? Amazon!
What comes to mind when you think of an home improvement/hardware? Home Depot!
What comes to mind when you think of cola? Coke! (This is part of the explanation for why Coke
has outsold and out-earned Pepsi just about every year, no matter how much Pepsi spends on
advertising. Coke was first and owns the cola position in the consumer’s mind.)

-- 5 of 16 --


The Makepeace Business System
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
What about desktop software for your PC? Microsoft!
What about copiers? Xerox.
Bleach? Clorox.
Energy drinks? Red Bull.
You get the point. Ries and Trout’s argument is essentially that the consumer thinks and sees things
in terms of categories, just like the ones we listed above.
This makes your marketing goal to OWN a category, so that when a consumer/prospect thinks of the
need/desire that’s fulfilled by that category, he immediately thinks of your product. First in his mind
usually makes it his first preference when making a purchase.
Also, once you’ve established this position – it’s darn near impossible to lose it. You almost have to try
to lose it with silly extensions that don’t fit your central positioning. For example, Xerox took a huge hit
when they tried to go into computers because it eroded their mental position as the leader in copiers.
The same happened to IBM when they tried to go into Xerox’s sector – sales of their computers were
pummeled.
And while Ries and Trout focus primarily on brand marketing – where consumer decisions are based
almost entirely on the positioning of the product – it also has powerful applications for direct response
marketers – whether you’re a copywriter, marketer or business owner.
Here are secrets I’ve discovered for making positioning work – whether you’re a copywriter, partner,
entrepreneur or anyone else …
Positioning Strategy #1:
Survival of the “FIRSTEST”
You see, you’ll often need to create ads for products in categories with heavy competition. For
example, the newsletter market is fiercely competitive. There are TONS of newsletters on natural
health, investments, and more.
With a prospect seeing promotions over and over again from a variety of editors, he becomes aware
of the category and thinks about this when the time comes to make his purchase decision. Being the
leader – the newsletter advisor that’s first in his mind – makes it much more likely he’ll buy from you.
The power of this positioning can sometimes supersede great copy and offers from competitors if
your positioning is strong enough.
So that’s the #1 strategy of positioning theory – BE FIRST.
For example, when I was first asked by Phillips to promote Dr. Julian Whitaker’s newsletter on
Alternative Health, the category was just beginning. But the promotion was so successful, Dr.
Whitaker became the #1 figure in the category – he was the one people immediately saw when they
thought of this type of product.
Other editors and newsletters have come in, but none could overtake Dr. Whitaker. His position was
simply too strong. When prospects thought of a natural health newsletter, they immediately thought of
Dr. Whitaker. Even when competitors come in, they make people more aware of the category at large

-- 6 of 16 --

7
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
and further bolster his position. In this way, some of THEIR marketing dollars are going to selling
newsletters for Dr. Whitaker!
This is one of the reasons why Ries and Trout believe that you should do whatever possible to avoid
being second in a category. You’ll work twice as hard and spend twice as much to make half as
many sales. You can overtake the #1 product/person sometimes, but it usually requires truckloads of
spending in advertising and marketing mistakes by whoever’s in that top tier position. This so rarely
succeeds. And there’s such high risk that it’s not really worth trying.
I refer to this as the “golden ladder” where products within a category have their place on a golden
ladder. This ladder is just a metaphor, and it exists entirely in your prospect’s mind.
When you’re first and sit atop the ladder, you’re almost invincible. Second’s okay, but it’s lots of work
with less payoff. And the rewards decrease exponentially as you go down the ladder.
Something to Be Careful About
The caveat to this is that being “first” isn’t always about timeline, it’s about perception and awareness.
That’s why you’ve also got to be very cautious about your ability to market and “roll out” your position.
Let me explain. Say that you’ve got a fantastic new idea for an energy drink. It contains a specific
form of vitamin C that actually gives you a rush of energy beyond anything else available.
So there’s no need for caffeine or other “harsh” stimulants.
You may decide to call it something like “Ener-C.” Whatever you decide to call it, the cool thing is that
you’ve just created a new category – vitamin C powered energy drinks. And since you’re first, you’re
going to own and dominate the market, right?
Not so fast!
What if you have a limited budget – and Coca Cola loves your idea. They decide they’re going to
create a similar drink – and then pour tens of millions of dollars into advertising it.
Now think about the vast majority of your prospects out there. Who are they going to think is the “first”
vitamin C powered energy drink?
Uh oh. You guessed it.
Your brilliant idea was lost – well, actually legally stolen by a much bigger fish.
You were technically first, but not in the majority of prospect’s minds – and so Coca Cola owns the
position.
So How Do You Counter This Problem?
Well, the first thing to be aware of is what this example teaches. Namely, that you need to be the first
to saturate your market with a position – not just to enter it.
The more people in your market to see your name first, the more the perception will be that you’re the
leader in that category.
And the harder it will be for someone else to steal that position.

-- 7 of 16 --


The Makepeace Business System
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
In terms of making sure you don’t get taken over when you have a great idea, it’s very much a
business and financial decision. You may have to raise capital so that you can launch big. Or
you may need to continually roll all of your sales and profits back into your marketing – MOVING
AGRESSIVELY – so that you can saturate your market before potential copycats rip off your position.
Bottom line: The solution is going to vary with every situation, but the principle here is something you
MUST be aware of when you’re partnering and consulting with your client. You don’t want him to push
forward and reveal his amazing new position if he’s not going to be able to saturate the market before
others.
Positioning Strategy #2:
Can’t Win Your Category?
Create Your OWN!
This is another skill you must master to be a great partner.
Rather than try to become #1 when you weren’t first – and this is where you come in as a copywriter
– Ries and Trout recommend you create your own category!
You build a new golden ladder in your prospect’s mind that instantly differentiates you from the leader.
You’re no longer battling uphill against an insurmountable foe – you’re sitting pretty atop your own
new golden ladder. And when prospect’s think of this new category you’ve created … PRESTO …
you’re the one they’ll go to!
One of the best examples of this is something copywriting superstar Arthur Johnson did for Dr. Bill
Douglass’ alternative health newsletter.
Whether consciously or instinctively, Arthur realized that it was foolish to go up against Dr. Whitaker
and others in the mainstream alternative health category. The sales potential on presenting natural
“cures” – just like Whitaker and the competition – was meager.
But Arthur spotted the fact that prospects had been growing more and more skeptical of the claims
made in alternative health promotions. They’d tried many of the solutions and few worked – and
hardly ever as well as they were promised. Plus, they were frustrated because much of the advice
had been centered around taking expensive supplements and doing things they didn’t enjoy – like
drinking lots of water, eating mostly vegetables, lots of exercising, and more.
So in his brilliance, Arthur created a new category all together! Dr. Douglass became the new
ladder in “Contrary Medicine” and got to stand on the top tier of his own new golden ladder. His
approach delivered REAL health solutions for all those fed up with both the natural and conventional
mainstream authorities.
Another great example of this is what I did with Dr. Martin Weiss in the late 1990’s.
You see, if you’ve ever gone through stacks of control mailings for the investment newsletter market
from the late nineties and early part of this century – what you’ll see is incredibly revealing...
Many of the claims in the headlines for these newsletters run together. None are truly unique and
almost every one of them simply offered a benefit like, “Make 456% In The Gold Bull Market!” or “7
Stocks You Must Own In 1999.” Nothing terribly unique, especially if you’re familiar with the market.

-- 8 of 16 --


www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
Plus, times were starting to change. The Tech Wreck, the Blue Chip Bust, Earnings Scandals and
other devastating market swings had gobbled up massive amounts of investor wealth and many were
feeling incredibly uneasy about investing at the time.
Recognizing this profound fear and distrust, I reasoned that what investors wanted more than
anything was an advocate. Someone that could warn them about the coming market swings before
they happened, expose the blatant lies that cost them fortunes – and someone that was savvy
enough to help them make a buck as these markets went haywire.
With that insight, I carefully positioned Martin Weiss as the #1 advocate of investor safety. He became
the passionate Nostradamus of the financial market – someone with almost frightfully good forecasts.
Especially when it came to disasters that threatened investors’ safety. Each promotion became an
extension of this central position and lifted Dr. Weiss well above the other competition. It was like a
whole new category and ladder had been born.
Did it work? Boy, did it ever! In fact, while others were mailing 200,000 pieces a month in their
heyday, Dr. Weiss was mailing 2 MILLION! That’s the power of positioning.
So take a close look at your product or service relative to the industry or market you’re in. Do
prospects think of you first? Second? Third? Not at all?
Start thinking about how you can change that. What new category might you create that you could
be first in? You’re essentially looking for a need or desire and then attempting to create a specific
category that caters to that desire. That way, if you can be first, your prospects will immediately
associate YOU with that need!
Positioning Strategy #3:
Avoid Stupid Line Extensions!
In Positioning, Trout and Ries also warn about the limitations that positioning places on a company’s
product line. They pointed out that if customers have been taught to believe that you make the
world’s best toothpaste, you don’t attempt to extend your line by introducing a new hemorrhoid
remedy.
Seems like a no-brainer to me! And yet many writers and marketers have fallen into the trap of using
established editors of investment newsletters to introduce a vitamin product, for example. Since the
investment guru has no credibility concerning health topics in the prospect’s mind, these kinds of line
extensions are destined for failure.
But it gets worse. MUCH worse. When you foolishly extend your product line into a totally new
category, you don’t just set your new product line for failure. Nope.
What happens is that by promoting this extension into a new category, you weaken your ownership of
your original position. This jeopardizes your spot on the golden ladder and can seriously damage the
bread and butter of your business.
Ries and Trout have TONS of examples of huge companies, with otherwise phenomenal growth, who
sabotaged everything by extending into the wrong areas.
For example, perhaps you’re the leading online retailer of golf videos. When somebody comes online
to buy a golf video, you’re the go-to guy. Business is going well. You’re hot and profitable. But maybe

-- 9 of 16 --

10
The Makepeace Business System
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
sales plateau after a little while. And you start to get antsy.
You realize that you can use your capital, business knowledge, and systems to conquer another
profitable market: Tennis. And this is where most get greedy: You also know that you can ride your
track record, name recognition and credibility from the golf industry into this new one. People already
know and respect you, so sales should be easy – right?
Not necessarily!
What usually happens is that new prospects in the new category think, “Hey this guy may be an
expert on golf, but not on tennis. How can he be both?”
And then old, previously loyal prospects – who thought of you as the ultimate expert in golf – now
wonder how much of an expert you can be in both businesses at the same time. And as a result, you
eat crow on both ends.
Your new venture is limited by your old position and image, and your original business suffers
because the association isn’t as strong as it once was.
And the solution is simple: FOCUS. If you want to go into a new category, create a new company,
editor or product. This is what Phillips did with its health department – which is now called Healthy
Directions. And it’s quite successful. Or if you’re a financial editor and you want to sell nutritional
supplements, find a new editor with a unique position that matches what your prospects are looking
for.
Before I finish this – let me just state that there ARE exceptions to this. And sometimes you can
combine two very seemingly unrelated subjects through a single position. For example, The Bob
Livingston Letter successfully addresses both health and investment issues. And despite the fact that
health and investment are very different topics – they’re connected through a core position of the “we
give you other side of the story – what the fat cats won’t tell you” …
Basically, the publication focuses on real-life conspiracies that threaten the prospect … the cover-ups
and dangers that could ruin his health and finances.
Positioning Strategy #4:
Using the Power of Focus to Overcome Tough Times
Okay, so you now see how FOCUS is a critical factor in enhancing one’s position. And why losing
your focus – through unrelated line extensions – can cost you big time.
But the focus can do even more for you. In times when your market has gone sour and your
prospects no longer believe that your category can fulfill their needs, you can use the power of focus
to break things wide open.
Let’s relate this to a real trend: A few years ago, something very much like this was happening in
the alternative health industry. Health conscious people had tried all the alternative cures and had
been disappointed by many of them. As a result, response rates for health newsletter promotions fell
precipitously.
Response rates fell so low in fact, that most publishers could only afford to promote to their own files.
Almost nobody was able to field successful subscription promotions to rented mailing lists.

-- 10 of 16 --

11
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
Up until that time, most promotions for health newsletters were still patterned after the original Health
& Healing promotions I had created in the early 1990s. Each of these promotions presented dozens
of “amazing” natural remedies that were cheaper and that the editor believed to be safer and more
effective than drugs.
But by the late 1990s and early 2000s, the prospects for these types of promotions had seen it all.
Too many of the “cures” hadn’t worked for them. And response rates began dropping like a rock.
But a few ingenious marketing people and copywriters did find a way to produce home-runs, even
in that discouraging environment. How? By REPOSITIONING their editors and products as more
skeptical analysts of alternative remedies. Suddenly, we began seeing headlines like ...
“Had Enough?”
... Enough Turkey Burgers and Sprouts? Enough Drinking Water Until You’re Ready to Burst?
*****
“Why Bilberry and Lutein Don’t Work!”
*****
“I Haven’t Had a Glass of Water in Over 20 years …”
*****
“Tell the Health Police to Take a Flying Leap! And return to life’s guiltiest pleasures …”
BRILLIANT! By understanding their prospects’ disappointments and frustrations with the very
products they were assigned to promote – and then repositioning their editors and newsletters so as
to connect with those feelings in a powerful way – a select handful of copywriters were able to create
powerful new controls in an otherwise dismal environment.
Now think: How do your prospects view the products or services you’re writing about? How are
your products or services positioned in their minds now? How can you reposition your product – by
adopting a narrower focus – in a way that’s a better fit with your prospect’s desires and fears? In other
words, how can you step away from your competition so that what they claim/promise becomes much
less important?
Do this enough and you’ll begin to develop x-ray vision for how prospects in your industry think, feel
and see products. And you’ll have a vastly superior ability to focus and reposition your product against
others, leaving them in the dust.
Examples of Positioning Throughout
the Direct Response Publishing Industry
As we mentioned earlier in this report, positioning is generally regarded as a branding concept. And
that’s why so few direct response marketers actually study it.
But that’s not smart, in our opinion. The power of positioning – and the breakthroughs you’ll gain
by understanding it – are every bit as valuable in this industry. And to drive the point of positioning
home, I’d like you to think about the positioning of some of the different products and services that are
promoted through direct response …

-- 11 of 16 --

12
The Makepeace Business System
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
■ The Bob Livingston Letter – The #1 way to get the other side of the story. (The one
that protects you against the assaults on your health and wealth. Secrets for bucking
the system and getting healthier and wealthier.)
■ Weiss – The #1 advocate of investor safety. (One of the most accurate forecaster of
wrecks, disasters and booms. Perhaps the most credible advisor in the industry.)
■ Dr. Dave Williams – #1 Natural Cure Finder. (He’s the “cure hunter” who travels the
world searching for natural breakthroughs, to bring them to you first.)
■ Dr. Julian Whitaker – The Original, #1 Alternative Medicine Doctor/Editor. (The original
alternative health editor who’s always willing to battle the FDA on your behalf.)
■ Dr. Sinatra – #1 Alternative Heart Doctor. (Highly credible heart specialist who
combines prescription drugs with nutritional therapy and supplements.)
■ Dr. Douglas – #1 Contrary Medical Doctor. (The brutally honest and funny, myth
busting MD who gives you the best in contrarian medicine.)
■ Jay Abraham – The world’s highest-paid marketing consultant. (His creative
breakthroughs have generated over $8 billion for his clients.)
Positioning Strategy #5:
Reverse-Engineer What Others Are Doing
Up until this point, I’ve told you why positioning works … its importance … I’ve given you great
examples. But I still haven’t told you exactly how to do it yourself.
That’s where this step and the next one come in.
You see, positioning isn’t as easy to teach as other marketing concepts. It’s more challenging, and
requires greater creativity than writing a bullet … or a guarantee … or closing copy.
And there’s no exact formula you can give someone for it. A big part of it is intuitive.
But here’s an exercise I’ve discovered to make the intuitive part easier. It’s simple: Every time you see
a successful product or advertisement ask yourself, “What’s the positioning here?”
More importantly, the more you study successful examples, the more you’ll sharpen your “positioning
muscle.” And that will give you the ability to adapt your skills better, with a far greater degree of
intuition than someone who tries to rely on formula.
Again, positioning is not a “plug and play” type of skill. It requires intense mental effort to build the skill
… and then intense mental effort to apply the skill.
Which brings us to the next point …

-- 12 of 16 --

13
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
Positioning Strategy #6:
Rub Up Against Your Market
Of course, it’s not enough to just understand positioning or see how it’s making others successful
– you’ve got to also be able to do it for your own products, clients, partners … you’ve got to make it
work for you!
And the best way I know to do that is to rub up intensely against your market. By that I mean, you’ve
got to fully immerse yourself in the market you’re selling into. You should know ALL your legitimate
competition … you should be keenly aware of promises they’re making … you should try their
products … you should talk extensively with prospects.
And you should do it all within the context of positioning. This is above and beyond the normal
research you perform.
Why all the hard work? No shortcuts?
Unfortunately, this is one of those areas where true creativity is directly related to the proper
expenditure of effort. The more you understand how everyone else is positioning themselves
– what they’re claiming and communicating to your prospects – the more you’ll spot the “window” of
opportunity.
This “window” will be an all-new position that separates you from others and sets you up for maximum
attention and sales.
And this process pertains to products, to your spokesperson, your company – anything you need to
stand out and be different in a meaningful way.
Get it? The more you study your particular niche within the context and mindset of positioning, the
easier it will be for you to come up with that big breakthrough.
Now let’s look at a really cool case …
A Fun Example of Positioning:
The Rich Jerk
I couldn’t help but laugh when I first saw this promotion. Or be amazed by the certain genius of it. It’s
for an Internet marketing product based around a character called the Rich Jerk. And it’s a fantastic
example of how grabbing a unique position – even one you think would be totally offensive – can
differentiate you and work like a charm.
Here’s the opening paragraph of copy on his homepage:
Let’s get something straight, I am a jerk. I am obnoxious. I am lazy. And I don’t care,
because I am FILTHY RICH. I am much too important to even be sitting here writing this
right now. So I’ll keep it short. I make several million dollars every year on the Internet,
doing almost nothing. Heard that before? Well you can see proof of my earnings.
Have you seen proof like that anywhere else? Didn’t think so. Do you make that much
money? Didn’t think so.

-- 13 of 16 --

1
The Makepeace Business System
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
Notice how shockingly different this approach is. And since no one else is saying anything like this,
the Rich Jerk completely owns the category of obnoxious millionaire marketing gurus.
Of course, not everybody will respond to this kind approach. Many people won’t take it seriously or
will just be upset that he’s saying things like this. But others will see it as being brutally honest and
therefore MORE trustworthy. After all, they’re not looking for a new friend, they’re looking to make
money and get RICH as quickly and easily as possible. If this guy can help them, that’s all that
matters.
That’s something else to remember about positioning – don’t be afraid to polarize your audience a bit.
You’re not looking to appeal to everyone! Rather, you want the most targeted group of prospects to
recognize you as the best possible solution to their desires.
It continues:
I’m the real deal. Whether you like it or not, you want to be rich like me, otherwise you
wouldn’t be here. And since you found this website, I’m sure you already know that
there are thousands of “get rich quick” programs on the Internet today. You’ve probably
even bought some of them yourself. Well, are you impressed with them? I’m not. Not
even close. What proof do these losers have of their earnings? That they make a few
thousand dollars a month? Big deal. I make millions. And we all know that the only
way these “gurus” make any money is from selling some ridiculous kit full of DVDs and
books about how to get rich. It’s pathetic. I make millions, and it’s not from selling some
lame 14-hour course. My income is so automatic, it would make you sick.
Here he’s tapping into the emotions of people who’ve tried all kinds of programs that don’t work,
especially within the context of credibility. He even provides a link to his receipts so the reader can
see how much money he makes.
So who the Hell am I? I’m the guy you see driving down the street in a Ferrari with the
top down, and you think to yourself, “What an ass!” And you know what? You’re right.
But so what? Do you really think I value your opinion? I’m a rich jerk, so I couldn’t care
less. I cut through the BS. If you find me offensive, obnoxious, or whatever … feel free
to leave my website at anytime. I’m not here to make friends. I’m here to show you
EXACTLY how I make millions. It’s no secret that I’m selling something here. And when
you see what I’m offering, you will be blown away.
Again, he reinforces his position as a jerk who doesn’t care. He’s just about helping you make more
money. He’s not here to make friends, he’s here to share his specific system for making money.
What’s also interesting is that, in all likelihood, his program has little that’s truly unique or different
than others on Internet marketing. I mean, it costs $40 – how superior could it really be? But that
doesn’t matter, because he’s chosen such a strong and unique position, he’s able to offer the same
stuff as anyone else and make lots of sales, fund infomercials, etc.
In fact, this distinction really helps drive home a powerful point …
The Final Secret:
Don’t Forget to Differentiate YOURSELF!
The same also goes for you if you’re a freelance copywriter or marketer. You need to start thinking of

-- 14 of 16 --

15
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
The #1 Most Overlooked Skill for Getting Rich in Direct Marketing
ways to position yourself as unique – and advantageous to clients. The more you can stand out, the
better off you’ll be. (You want to stand out in a positive way! It goes without saying that anybody can
stand out negatively.)
Sometimes this just takes putting more personality into your self-promotions. In fact, if you’re just
beginning and don’t have a killer track record – it may be the ONLY way! Let’s say you have two
copywriters vying for the same job. Both have about the same experience, both have started to write
winners and make money for their clients, both have samples that are equally good – who’s going to
get the job? All else being relatively equal, it’s the person with the most uniquely pleasant personality
that’s going to win out.
It might mean displaying candor in your copy … or revealing something very personal about yourself
(a fear, a worry, a challenging experience – or the secret reason why you became a copywriter) … the
possibilities are endless.
Why does this work? Because, someone who’s done this has differentiated himself. He’s given the
marketing director an enjoyable experience – one that builds greater rapport and trust. So when it
comes time to make a decision between these two equally qualified candidates, there really is no
decision.
It’s clear that one writer or marketer really “gets” the importance of positioning and uniqueness at
a deep level, while the other doesn’t. So don’t be the other guy. Use these secrets to differentiate
yourself and quickly gain the edge over anyone else you ever come up against.
Now let’s review the strategies we’ve discussed by going over your “Overnight Marketing Genius”
checklist on the next page …
